Command.Name: __default_command
Command.Description: dotnet-check-updates checks for possible upgrades of NuGet packages in C# projects. By default, dotnet-check-updates will search the current directory for C# project (.csproj) files. If no project files are found, it will then search for solution (.sln) files in the current directory.

Option.Description: Max number of concurrent HTTP requests. Minimum is 1 and maximum is 32. (default: 8)
Sets the current working directory. (default: current directory)
Recursion depth. Only applied when --recurse is also specified. Minimum is 1 and maximum is 16. Set to 0 to search arbitrary directory depth. (default: 4)
Exclude package names matching the given string, glob or comma-or-space-delimited list. Example: System* Aliases: -E, --reject, --exc
Include only package names matching the given string, glob or comma-or-space-delimited list. Example: System* Aliases: -I, --include, --inc
Enable interactive prompts for each package. Implies --upgrade.
Lists current packages and their versions even if they have no upgrade available. (default: false)
Do not show progress bar when fetching package information.

Specifies the URI of the NuGet package source to use during the check. This setting overrides all of the sources specified in the nuget.config files. Multiple sources can be provided by specifying this option multiple times.
Sets the number of items per page when running in interactive mode. Has no effect unless --interactive is specified. (default: 10)
Path to a .csproj file to search for upgrades. Exclusive with --solution. (default: searches for .csproj files in the current directory)
Shows absolute paths when printing projects and solutions. (default: false)
Shows number of packages found in each project after filtering has been applied
Path to a .sln file to search for projects. Exclusive with --project. (default: searches for .sln files in the current directory if no .csproj files are found)
Determines the version to upgrade to: latest, greatest, major, minor, patch, pre-major, pre-minor, pre-patch. (default: latest)
Upgrades packages to the appropriate version based on the current --target option. (default: false)
Shows the version of the application. Exits after outputting the version.
